title: दुर्गा अष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री दुर्गा अष्टकम् ॥

विश्वेश्वरीं जगद्धात्रीं स्थितिसंहारकारिणीम्।
निद्रां भगवतीं विष्णोरतुलां तेजसः प्रभाम्॥१॥

त्वं स्वाहा त्वं स्वधा त्वं हि वषट्कारः स्वरात्मिका।
सुधा त्वमक्षरे नित्ये त्रिधा मात्रात्मिका स्थिता॥२॥

अर्धमात्रास्थिता नित्या यानुच्चार्या विशेषतः।
त्वमेव सा त्वं सावित्री त्वं देवजननी परा॥३॥

त्वयैतद्धार्यते विश्वं त्वयैतत्सृज्यते जगत्।
त्वयैतत्पाल्यते देवि त्वमत्स्यन्ते च सर्वदा॥४॥

विसृष्टौ सृष्टिरूपा त्वं स्थितिरूपा च पालने।
तथा संहृतिरूपान्ते जगतोऽस्य जगन्मये॥५॥

महाविद्या महामाया महामेधा महास्मृतिः।
महामोहा च भवती महादेवी महेश्वरी॥६॥

प्रकृतिस्त्वं च सर्वस्य गुणत्रयविभाविनी।
कालरात्रिर्महारात्रिर्मोहरात्रिश्च दारुणा॥७॥

त्वं श्रीस्त्वमीश्वरी त्वं ह्रीस्त्वं बुद्धिर्बोधलक्षणा।
लज्जा पुष्टिस्तथा तुष्टिस्त्वं शान्तिः क्षान्तिरेव च॥८॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
दुर्गाष्टकमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेत्सततं नरः।
सर्वान् कामानवाप्नोति दुर्गादेवीप्रसादतः॥

॥ इति श्री दुर्गा अष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Durga Ashtakam ||

Vishveshwarim Jagaddhatrim Sthiti-samhara-karinim.
Nidram Bhagavatim Vishnor Atulam Tejasah Prabham.||1||

Tvam Svaha Tvam Svadha Tvam Hi Vashatkarah Svaratmika.
Sudha Tvam Akshare Nitye Tridha Matratmika Sthita.||2||

Ardha-matra-sthita Nitya Ya Anucharya Visheshatah.
Tvam Eva Sa Tvam Savitri Tvam Deva-janani Para.||3||

Tvayaitad Dharyate Vishvam Tvayaitat Srijyate Jagat.
Tvayaitat Palyate Devi Tvam Atsyante Cha Sarvada.||4||

Visrishtau Srishti-rupa Tvam Sthiti-rupa Cha Palane.
Tatha Samhriti-rupante Jagato'sya Jaganmaye.||5||

Mahavidya Mahamaya Mahamedha Mahasmritih.
Mahamoha Cha Bhavati Mahadevi Maheshwari.||6||

Prakritis Tvam Cha Sarvasya Guna-traya-vibhavini.
Kalaratrir Maharatrir Moharatris Cha Daruna.||7||

Tvam Shris Tvam Ishwari Tvam Hris Tvam Buddhir Bodha-lakshana.
Lajja Pushtis Tatha Tushtis Tvam Shantih Kshantir Eva Cha.||8||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Durga-ashtakam Idam Punyam Yah Pathet Satatam Narah.
Sarvan Kaman Avapnoti Durga-devi-prasadatah.

|| Iti Shri Durga Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
विश्वेश्वरी, जगद्धात्री, स्थिति और संहार करने वाली, विष्णु की निद्रा रूपा, अतुल तेजस्विनी भगवती को नमस्कार।

आप स्वाहा हैं, स्वधा हैं, वषट्कार हैं, स्वरात्मिका हैं। आप अक्षर सुधा, नित्या हैं, त्रिधा मात्रात्मिका हैं। आप ही सावित्री हैं, आप ही देवजननी परा हैं।

आपसे यह विश्व धारण होता है, आपसे सृष्टि होती है, आपसे पालन होता है, आप ही अन्त में सबका संहार करती हैं।

सृष्टि में सृष्टिरूपा, पालन में स्थितिरूपा और अन्त में संहृतिरूपा — हे जगन्मये!

आप महाविद्या, महामाया, महामेधा, महास्मृति, महामोहा, महादेवी, महेश्वरी हैं। आप सबकी प्रकृति हैं, गुणत्रय (सत्व, रजस्, तमस्) की विभाविनी हैं।

आप श्री, ईश्वरी, ह्री, बुद्धि, लज्जा, पुष्टि, तुष्टि, शान्ति और क्षान्ति हैं।

जो मनुष्य इस दुर्गाष्टक को सदा पढ़ता है, वह दुर्गादेवी की कृपा से सब कामनाएँ प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Invokes Durga as the supreme cosmic power - creator, sustainer, destroyer
Invokes all desires through the grace of goddess durga
Traditionally recited for removal of great delusion (mahamoha) and grants wisdom
Invokes the blessing of peace, patience, prosperity, and contentment
Powerful prayer from Devi Mahatmyam tradition
Invokes protection during kalaratri and removes all fears
description: Dedicated to Goddess Durga, the Durga Ashtakam (दुर्गा अष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn revered in Hindu tradition. Rooted in Devi Mahatmyam (Markandeya Purana), this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant all desires through the grace of Goddess Durga as well as invoke Durga as the supreme cosmic power - creator, sustainer, destroyer. Additionally, it is known to remove great delusion (mahamoha) and grant wisdom. This prayer is traditionally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri and Dussehra to invoke divine grace. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Durga Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Durga Ashtakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight powerful verses glorifying Goddess Durga as the supreme cosmic power.

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of the Durga Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Durga Ashtakam is rooted in the Devi Mahatmyam tradition of the Markandeya Purana, the supreme Shakta text that narrates the goddess Durga's three great battles against the asuras Madhu-Kaitabha, Mahishasura, and Shumbha-Nishumbha. Within this larger framework several eight-verse Durga praise hymns have circulated under the title Durga Ashtakam, some attributed to Adi Shankaracharya and others compiled by later Pauranika acharyas. The most popular version praises Durga as the slayer of Mahishasura, the supreme refuge of devas and rishis, and the embodiment of victory, protection and liberation. (Sources: Devi Mahatmyam; Hindupedia Durga; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Durga depicted with a lion or tiger as her vahana?
seoFaqs.answer: Durga's vahana is traditionally a lion (in some Pauranika sources, a tiger), gifted to her by Himavan when she manifested to slay Mahishasura. The lion symbolises uncontested sovereignty, fearlessness, and dharmic kingship — qualities Durga assumes as Maha-Devi when she leads the cosmic order against adharma. Riding the lion she carries weapons given by each of the gods: Vishnu's discus, Shiva's trident, Indra's thunderbolt, Vayu's bow, Surya's quiver, Yama's staff, Varuna's conch, Brahma's water-pot, Vishvakarma's axe and Himavan's lion. The Durga Ashtakam praises this iconography in its dhyana verses, evoking her as Simha-vahini, the lion-mounted goddess. (Sources: Devi Mahatmyam; Hindupedia Durga; Wikipedia.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Durga Ashtakam most auspiciously recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday and Friday are Durga's traditional weekdays. The supreme annual occasions are the nine nights of Sharad Navaratri in Ashvin (September-October) culminating in Vijayadashami (Dussehra), and the nine nights of Chaitra Navaratri in Chaitra (March-April) culminating in Rama Navami. The eighth night, Durga Ashtami, is particularly auspicious for the Ashtakam since both share the number eight. Other recommended occasions include Ashada and Magha Gupt Navaratras, Sankashti Chaturthi, Sankranti days, and any morning when the devotee seeks Durga's protection in difficult undertakings. The Ashtakam is also commonly recited before military service, examinations, court appearances and journeys. (Sources: Drikpanchang Navratri; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Durga Ashtakam promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti tradition associated with Durga ashtakams promises: removal of all obstacles in dharmic undertakings, protection from enemies and adversaries (shatru-bhaya-nashana), fearlessness in moments of crisis, victory in litigation and competition, freedom from chronic disease and untimely death, blessings of progeny and marital harmony, prosperity through Mahalakshmi's grace embedded in Durga, and ultimately moksha for the devoted reciter. The compact eight-verse format allows for daily recitation during Navaratri vrats and intense spiritual practice periods, with each repetition multiplying the merit. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Sanatan Sanstha.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Durga Ashtakam different from Mahishasura Mardini Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mahishasura Mardini Stotra (Ayi Giri Nandini), attributed to Adi Shankaracharya, is a longer 21-verse hymn with a distinctive rhythmic cadence praising Durga specifically as the slayer of Mahishasura, focusing on her battle iconography. The Durga Ashtakam is a more compact eight-verse stuti with broader thematic scope — covering her cosmological role, protective grace, maternal compassion, and liberating power in addition to her warrior aspect. Both are recited during Navaratri; the Mahishasura Mardini Stotra is preferred for its musical recitation tradition (it is set to specific Carnatic and Hindustani ragas), while the Ashtakam is preferred for compact daily Devi worship. (Sources: Hindupedia; Wikipedia Mahishasura Mardini Stotra; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Durga Ashtakam suitable for daily household recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. The eight-verse format takes only four to six minutes at devotional pace, making it ideal for inclusion in morning and evening household puja. Many Bengali, Maithili, Odia, Maharashtrian and South Indian households recite it daily before the Devi alcove. Children are commonly taught the Ashtakam from age seven onwards as a foundational Devi prayer. The stotra requires no special initiation, only cleanliness, an east or north-facing seat, an image or yantra of Durga, a lit lamp and devotional intention. During Navaratri the daily count is often increased to nine or 108 recitations as part of vrat-anushthana. (Sources: Sanatan Sanstha; Hindupedia; Drikpanchang Navratri.)
